steps.title: Identify the relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: The first sentence says the 2006 World Cup had 32 teams from six continents. The second sentence says the 1958 World Cup had only 16 teams from two regions. These two facts move in opposite directions — global scope vs. regional scope — so the blank needs a contrast transition.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Therefore,’: signals a consequence or logical result. The 1958 team count does not follow as a result of the 2006 count. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘For instance,’: signals an example. The 1958 tournament is not offered as an example of the 2006 claim. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In contrast,’: signals an opposition. The shift from 32 teams across six continents to only 16 from two regions is a direct contrast in scale and geographic reach. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘In other words,’: signals a restatement of the same idea. The two sentences say different things, not the same thing rephrased. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
